issues.claim: buddhi-yoga, the discriminating intelligence operating with equanimity
issues.objection: The engineering rendering folds buddhi-yoga toward the Shankara reading (jñāna-mature equanimity). Prabhupada/Gaudiya Vaishnava reads it as devotional-service-consciousness, where the buddhi is yoked specifically to Krishna. The source pack records this disagreement carefully; the engineering body itself simplifies toward the Shankara/Ramanuja side. Acceptable simplification (the doctrinal floor — 'discriminating intelligence operating with equanimity' — is what all four traditions converge on), but a careful Sanskritist notes that 'buddhi' here in Gaudiya readings is theistic-buddhi, not generic discriminating intelligence.

notes: The engineering layer correctly preserves the explicit ranking: dūreṇa avaram (by far inferior) is rendered as 'far below buddhi-yoga' / 'stands far below', preserving Krishna's tonal harshness. The closing tag kṛpaṇāḥ phala-hetavaḥ is rendered exactly as the source pack scopes it ('not pitied for failing; pitied for binding themselves through their own causality') — this matches both Shankara's phala-tṛṣṇā reading and Ramanuja's structural-misery reading. Sanskrit terms (buddhi-yoga, kṛpaṇa, phala-hetavaḥ, śaraṇam) appear in the engineering body without being mistranslated. The 'śaraṇam' in 'buddhau śaraṇam' is correctly handled — the engineering layer reads it as 'take refuge in buddhi' (matching all four traditions) and the source pack flags the cross-reference to 18.66 (sarva-dharmān parityajya) without conflating the two. One minor framing note on the Gaudiya Vaishnava simplification; not critical. Pass.
